step_by_step_thinking:
Transferring objects from one hand to the other demonstrates manipulation skills and awareness of spatial relationships, aligning well with cognitive development stages described by Piaget, particularly the sensorimotor period. Given the context of developmental milestones, transferring items represents a fine-motor activity indicating coordination and problem-solving related to grasping and releasing objects — critical aspects of tactile exploration and cognitive processing during early childhood.
